description:
Recto (original use): Power of attorney. Location: Alexandria. Dated: 16 Ḥeshvan 4835 AM = 9 November 1074 CE. A woman, Nājiya bt. Shelomo b. Hiba al-Miṣrī, the wife of Abū Saʿd Yiṣḥaq b. Khalaf al-Miṣrī, had previously appointed Abū Sahl Menashshe b. Moshe al-Qarawī as her agent to help her collect her property from her mother Ḥusna and sister Sittūna (she had moved from Alexandria to the Maghrib and left some of her property with them). The original power of attorney was drawn up in Zuwaylat al-Mahdiyya in Tammuz 4834 AM = June/July 1074 CE and is copied here verbatim, along with the names of all of its witnesses and validating judges (see PGPID 40245). Here, the agent Menashshe appoints his own agent, the parnas ʿEli b. Yaʿīsh ha-Kohen. Among those who signed the deed: Shela b. Mevasser, (Information in part from Frenkel.) 

Written on a single rotulus containing several legal documents in the handwriting of a single scribe, including a trousseau document (1079 CE) and documents in settlement of a business partnership.
description_he: יפוי כח. מיקום: אלכסנדריה. מתוארך: 16 חשון 4835 = 9 נובמבר 1074. נג'יה בת שלמה ב' היבה, מינתה בעבר את מנשה ב' משה כשלוחה לגבות רכושה מאמה ואחותה. כאן מנשה ממנה שליח-משנה: עלי ב' יאיש הכהן.
